headline: 19 contracts catalogued across Ethereum, Plume Mainnet, and Solana; 7 EVM admin/oracle contracts and 12 token/infrastructure contracts

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search for 'Superstate' surfaced primary USTB token contract (0x43415eB6ff9DB7E26A15b704e7A3eDCe97d31C4e) with proxy pattern confirmed.

Official deployments doc at https://docs.superstate.com/investors/smart-contracts.md lists 7 Ethereum mainnet contracts, 7 Sepolia testnet contracts, 3 Plume mainnet contracts, and 3 Plume testnet contracts by role.
Audit PDFs referenced in smart contracts page; 0xmacro audits (11 total) and Zellic audit confirm contract scope including USTB token, AllowList, and RedemptionIdle contracts.

GitHub repo superstateinc/ustb confirmed as official source; deployment changelog references mainnet.json commit dbd126e. Second repo superstateinc/onchain-redemptions identified for redemption functionality.
Multi-chain enumeration completed: Ethereum mainnet (7 contracts), Plume mainnet (3 contracts), Solana mainnet (4 contracts identified plus 2 Pyth oracles).
No factory contracts discovered; Superstate operates direct token deployments rather than factory patterns.
Contracts classified as token (USTB/USCC), admin (AllowlistV3), oracle (Continuous Price Oracle, Chainlink feeds), and redemption (RedemptionIdle) roles using controlled vocabulary.
All 19 addresses trace to fetched documentation (smart contracts page) and verified block explorer pages.

rationale.verdict: Comprehensive enumeration completed across three chains using official documentation, GitHub repositories, and block explorer verification. Ethereum mainnet, Plume mainnet, and Solana mainnet deployments all discovered and catalogued with labelled roles.

evidence.shows: Official smart contracts documentation listing all deployed contract addresses by chain and role, including Ethereum mainnet (7 contracts), Plume mainnet (3 contracts), and Solana mainnet (4 contracts plus Pyth oracles). Includes audit links and repository references.
Official GitHub repository for USTB token and AllowList contracts; contains Solidity source code, deployment scripts, and changelog documenting mainnet deployments at commit dbd126e and others.
Superstate GitHub organization page confirming verified domain control (superstate.co) and listing ustb and onchain-redemptions repositories as public projects.
Etherscan page for USTB token proxy showing EIP-1967 transparent proxy pattern with implementation at 0x1f50a1ee0ec8275d0c83b7bb08896b4b47d6e8c4.

0xMacro audit confirming redemption contracts and token functionality; references onchain-redemptions repository commits 7187bdfa59dab79cfba58dd341aa7a946b45771e and 233128c10fd54852d7bbdcefb522e45085a59bab.
Official website homepage identifying Superstate as a tokenization platform with products USTB (Short Duration US Government Securities Fund) and USCC (US Crypto Carry Fund).

unknowns: D2: Sepolia testnet contracts (7 addresses) present in documentation but testnet scope not confirmed for production assessment
D4: GitHub deployment JSON files not directly fetched; commit SHAs referenced but complete file contents not verified
D5: Solana Pyth oracle feed IDs listed (FWDI/USD, GLXY/USD) but these are Pyth price feeds rather than Superstate-controlled contracts

protocol_metadata.about: Superstate USTB is a tokenized short-duration U.S. Treasury securities fund enabling qualified purchasers to earn Treasury yields on-chain. USTB is issued as ERC-20 tokens on Ethereum and SPL tokens on Solana, with continuous NAV pricing updating second-by-second. The protocol uses permissioned allowlist controls, a custom continuous price oracle, and atomic mint/redeem functionality via USDC integration to provide DeFi composability for institutional yield strategies.
